summary: No P0/P1 architecture or process acceptance blocker identified. The pr-merge-readiness run preserves the main boundaries I checked: run artifacts are declared non-methodology source, final readiness is forbidden until the readiness_report phase, human merge approval is still required, review agents are packet-bound/read-only, and the workflow remains a utility rather than a release runtime. The repeated review_control_rules/review_cycle blocks are a real maintainability risk, but I did not find a concrete contradiction, broken validator path, or false merge-ready claim that would make this PR unsafe to accept. ADR-0017 red-capture is not a blocker for this pr-merge-readiness gate because this workflow has no implementation phase.

findings.title: Review-control policy is duplicated across workflow YAML files instead of factored into one reusable policy artifact
evidence: run-artifacts/agentsflow/runs/2026-06-23-v0.2-pr-merge-readiness-daed76c/review-packets/architecture-codex.json declares WARN-WORKFLOW-COMPOSITION-001: common review-control policy is repeated across workflow YAML files rather than factored into one reusable profile/policy artifact.

workflows/big-feature-contract-first/workflow.yaml, workflows/review-only-fusion/workflow.yaml, workflows/pr-merge-readiness/workflow.yaml, and workflows/project-initialization/workflow.yaml each carry local review_control_rules/review_cycle blocks.

docs/review-fusion-model.md defines a reusable finding-control pipeline and states workflows should select review topology and provide context rather than duplicate the finding-control procedure locally.

The inspected duplicated blocks differ by workflow context, for example verification_gate versus evidence_gate and different materiality triggers, but I did not find a contradictory acceptance rule or validator bypass in the current artifacts.
findings.why_it_matters: This can drift as future edits touch one workflow policy block but not the others. In this PR it remains a non-blocking factoring issue because the shared docs, validators, and current pr-merge-readiness packet preserve the mandatory gates, candidate-finding lifecycle, human authority boundary, and freshness rules.
findings.recommendation: Track a follow-up to factor shared review-cycle invariants into a reusable profile or policy artifact, leaving only workflow-specific deltas in workflow.yaml.
findings.no_blocker_path_reason: No current acceptance break found: repository validation evidence is green in the packet, pr-merge-readiness does not claim merge_ready, and the repeated blocks do not contradict the human-decision or candidate-finding rules I inspected.

requests_for_additional_verification.request: Before final merge-ready status, ensure the final readiness report includes completed live provider-mirrored review evidence, finding validation, and a human merge decision bound to material_change_id daed76c and the final report hash.

self_declared_limitations: Read-only review only: I inspected files and git metadata but did not run tests, execute scripts, modify files, create patches, or update evidence.

I focused on architecture/process boundaries and sampled the large main..HEAD diff through source-of-truth docs, workflows, validators, run artifacts, and reviewer/provider paths rather than line-reviewing every added artifact.
Findings are candidate-unvalidated and should be handled by the main orchestrator's relevance-validation process before affecting acceptance.
